ety: S. नटः, rt. नट्, corr. fr. नृत्
def: s.m. (f. -ī, -in, -nī, -inī), A dancer; rope-dancer; tumbler; mime, actor; juggler; — name of a particular vagrant caste, the Indian gipsy (said to be the offspring of a degraded Kshatriya by a woman of the second caste; they are generally rope-dancers, jugglers, &c.); — name of a subordinate mode in music; — the tree Calosanthes indica; the tree Jonesia asoka; — a sort of reed, Arunda tibialis, or A. karka
more: s. m. The name of a tribe, who are generally jugglers, ropedancers, &c. a tumbler, mime, actor. ¶ (dakh.) Cheat, knave, deceiver, deceitful ( Harris ).
subs: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode (according to some, the sixth, or, according to others, a subdivision of that called dīpak, or of megh)
vulg. naṭ-bidyā, s.f. The art of rope-dancing, or tumbling, &c.

def: s.f. A description; an epithet; a descriptive epithet or phrase, a noun-adjective; — praise, eulogium, encomium (esp. of the prophet Moḥammad).
more: s. f. 1. Praise (especially, of Muḥammad ), eulogium. 2. An epithet. 3. An adjective noun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَعْت (na't), from Arabic نَعْت (naʕt).

def: adj. (dakh.) Alike, similar, like, analogous, even ( Harris ): see ناد nād.
s.f. (dialec.) A pillar; an obelisk; — an architrave; — a beam; a log (of wood).
more: s. (dakh.) Pillar, beam, architrave, log of wood: see لاٿهـ lāṭh.

def: s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
more: n. m. S. ज्ञातेय Relationship; relative; kindred ( rishtah , 2). 1. Nātā na gotā, khaṛā ho-kar rotā . Prov. Nor kith nor kin is left, he stands and weeps bereft. 2. Chhoṛe gāoṅ se nātā kyā? Prov. The village left, connection cleft. 3. Jag meṅ dekhat hī ko nāto . Prov. Relations in this world are but relations to the view. nātā ṭūṭnā , v. n. To be broken (connection). Nānī marī, nātā tūṭā . Prov. The grandmother dead, snapped kinship's thread. nātā-joṛnā , v. n. To form a connection with. nāte-dār , n. m. A relative; kinsman. nāte-dārī , n. f. Relationship ( rishte-dārī ).
adj. Short and stout; short; dwarfish. Nāṭā sab se ṭāṅṭā . Prov. In the short of size strength great lies. nāṭā , n. m. 1. A naughty boy. 2. An uncastrated young bull, not yet broken in.
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Short, dwarfish; dapper; — vile, depraved, vicious, bad, naughty; — a dwarf; — a depraved or bad man; a naughty boy; — a young bull not yet broken in.
s.f. A species of shrub, Caesalpina bonducella.
s.m. Dancing; a dance; a play, a pantomime; — the country called the Carnatic
subs: s.m. Dancing-saloon, ball-room; a room or hall for dramatic performances, a theatre.

def: part. (f. -a), Bent, bowed; inclined, inclining; crooked, curved; depressed, sunk
subs: (˚ta+an˚), adj. (f. -ī), Bowing the limbs; bending the body; bowing, bent, stooping; curved
or nat, s.m. (dialec.) Any turbinate univalve shell (with, or without, the creature in it); a snail; periwinkle, cockle, whelk, mussel, &c.
adj. & adv. & s.m. (f. -ī), Bending down the face, looking down
with the face down; — a person who looks down
or who has his face down.

def: s.m. Zenith-distance at meridian transit (of a planet); hour-angle, or the distance (of a planet) from the meridian
s. (dakh.) Deceit, fraud, knavery, guile ( Harris ).
m. plur. (of نتيجه) Results, consequences, issues, &c.
s.m. pl. (of natīja, q.v.), Fruits, consequences, issues, &c.

ety: nātā, q.v.+āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the aff. of the pres. part. with the caus. aug. āpi)
def: adj. & s.m. Related, kin; — a relation, relative, kinsman.

s.m. (f. -ikā, or -ī), A dancer; an actor; a mime; — dancing; acting; — a play, drama, comedy; the first of the ten species of Sanskrit dramatic compositions of the first order; — the business of a naṭ (q.v.)
more: s. m. 1. The business of a نٿ naṭ, q. v. 2. A kind of Sanskrit dramatick composition, a comedy.
subs: vulg. nāṭak-sālā, or nāṭak-sāl, s.f. A dancing-room, ball-room; a play-house, theatre (syn. nāć-ghar)
(prob. for nāṭak-sālī), s.m. & f. (Dakh.) A dancer, &c. (i.q. nāṭak).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit नाटक (nāṭaka).

def: n. m. A play; a drama. nāṭak-sāl, nāch-ghar , n. m. A theatre.
s.f. A dancing-girl; — a kind of dramatic writing.
adj. Dramatic; comic.
s. m. (dakh. i. q. بهگتيا) An actor (for entertainment), a player.
m. The beginning of a particular holy text.
s.m. (Dakh.) A great merchant; a respectable man; head-man (syn. mahājan).
more: s. m. (dakh.) A respectable or head man: see مـﮩاجن mahā-jan.

def: s.f. Denial; negation; refusal.
more: s. f. The female of نٿ naṭ, q. v.

def: adj. See s.v. nata or nat. s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

def: s.m. See s.v. nat. s.m. 1˚ = nātā, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of nāth, q.v. s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

ety: nāṭā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Shortness (of stature), dwarfishness.
conj. (dialec.) If not — then; else, otherwise (i.q. nā-to, or nā-tau, or nahīṅ to).
conj. (dialec.) = nātar or nātari, q.v. conj. (dialec.) If not — then; else, otherwise (i.q. nā-to, or nā-tau, or nahīṅ to).
s.f. The state or condition, or the office, of an actor, &c.; actorship.
s.m. = naṭ-bar, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. corr. of nā-ṭhaur, q.v. s.m. Chief dancer or actor; — dancer, rope-dancer, &c. (i.q. naṭ, q.v.).
s.m. Chief dancer or actor; — dancer, rope-dancer, &c. (i.q. naṭ, q.v.).
more: s. m. A juggler, &c. See نٿ naṭ.

def: Relationship, and Pers. دار Having) adj. Akin; s. m. f. Relation, relative.
v.t. To turn up (the eyes), to close (the eyes, in death).
v. n. To close one's eyes, or turn up the pupils as in death. Voh be-chārah āṅkheṅ naṭer gagā .

ety: Prk. नत्था; S. नस्ता; — cf. also S. नाथः, which is prob. a Prk. form
def: s.f. The nose-string of an ox (by which he is led, or which serves as reins); — a seton (cf. nāth, nathnā, nathnī)
subs: na āge nāth na pīćhe paghā
'Neither nose-string in front nor tether behind'; free to roam; without tie or encumbrance.

def: s.m. (f. -ā), Protector, patrou, master, lord; husband; — a common affix to the names of Hindū ascetics (e.g. Gorakh-nāth)
subs: nāth-vat, or nāth-want (f. -vatī), adj. Having a protector, or master; having a husband; dependent, subject (to; — syn. nāthī).

def: s.m. Non-existence.

n. f. Dim. nathlī, nathnī , S. नाथ A nose-ring. Ilāhī! terī nath, chūṛī bar-qarār raheṅ! Bencdiction. Heaven grant your nose-ring and your bangles you may ever wear! (Widows are not permitted to wear them). nath baṛhānā , v. n. To take off the nose-ring.
more: s. m. f. 1. A large ring worn in the nose (on the left nostril). 2. A rope passed through the nose of a draught ox.

def: s. m. Nonexistence.

n. m. S. नाथ; Pālī, nātho . 1. A lord; master. Kit murlī, kit chaṅdrkā, kit gopan kā sāth? Tulsī Dās. Tulsī! jan ke kārne Nāth bhaye Raghunāth . 2. A title borne by Jogīs . Baṛe pauṅhche hue nāth haiṅ . 3. A nose-string for beasts Nāth na sāṅṭā, thām merā nāṭā! Rus. Nor nose-string, nor a lash; just hold my ox, he says.
more: s. m. 1. A master, a husband, a lord. 2. Often used as an affix to the names of Jogīs; as, گورکهناتهـ gorakh-nāth. 3. s. f. The string in a bullockʼs nose. 4. A seton.
wety: From Sanskrit नाथ (nātha).

def: s.f. (or m.?) A rope passed through the nose of a draught-ox, nose-string (i.q. nāth, q.v.); — a large (gold or silver) ring worn (by women) in the nose (on the left nostril); nose-ring (widows are not allowed to wear either Lose-rings or bangles; hence, the benediction, ilāhī, terī nath-ćūṛī bar-qarār raheṅ)
subs: (apnī), To take off her nose-ring (a woman).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A corrupt form of nātā, q.v.
(Braj), s.f. pl. of nath, q.v.
s.f. = S ناتهتو नाथत्व nāthatva, s.m. The office or status of a protector, or master; protectorship; patronage, &c
s.m. = S ناتهتا नाथता nāthatā, s.f. The office or status of a protector, or master; protectorship; patronage, &c
adj. Having no fixed or proper place; not in (its) proper place, out of place (i.q. be-ṭhaur).
part. (of nāthnā), Having put a string in the nose (of an ox), i.q. nāth-ke.
adj. (f. -inī), Having a protector, or master; having a husband; dependent, subject (to).
adj. Having a hole bored in the septum of the nose, or having a string through its nose (an ox or other draught-animal).
s.m. (rustic) A vagabond; scoundrel; a term of abuse.
n. m. nā not, thiyā place. 1. One who has no fixed abode. 2. A vagabond; a scoundrel. 3. A term of abuse.
s.f. (dialec.) dim. of nath, and = nath or nathnī, qq.v. s.f. The nose-string of an ox (by which he is led, or which serves as reins); — a seton (cf. nāth, nathnā, nathnī)
subs: na āge nāth na pīćhe paghā
'Neither nose-string in front nor tether behind'; free to roam; without tie or encumbrance.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = nathnī, q.v. s.f. A nose-ring (i.q. nath, q.v.); — a small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword; — a string passed through (or tied round) a bullock's nose (by which to guide him)
subs: (-kī), To be dishonoured; to be violated (a virgin).

ety: fr. nāth, q.v.; or nāth˚ = S. नस्तित
def: v.t. To bore the nose (of a bullock) and put a string in it (to guide him by); — to bring under subjection or control.
more: v. a. S. नाथन 1. To put a string through a bullock's nose. Apnā bail kulhāṛī nāthab . Bhoj. Prov. My own ox's nose I can bore with an axe if I please. 2. To bring under control, subjugate.
v. a. To bore a bullockʼs nose and put a string in it to guide him by.

ety: nath, q.v.+nā = lā = Prk. अलओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A nostril; — a large ring for the nose
subs: (with gen.), To pierce (one's) nostrils with an arrow
(mere, or us-ke), 'The breath to come into (one's) nostrils,' to be worried, or plagued, or harassed
or nathne ćaṛhānā, To distend, or to turn up, the nostrils; to be enraged, or displeased
(merā or kisī-kā) nathnoṅ-tak ā-jānā, 'The soul to come as far as the nostrils'; to be ready to drop, or faint, or die (from exhaustion, or worry, &c.); to be worried to death, to be sorely tried.

def: v.n. To have the nose bored, and a string passed through it (a bullock).
more: n. m. A nostril. nathne phulānā , v. n. 1. To swell out one's nostrils. 2. ( nathne chaṛhānā ) To be enraged, etc. ( nāk chaṛhānā ). nathnoṅ kī phaṛak , n. f. Movement of the nostrils. nath'nī , n. f. A small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword. nathnī utarnā , v. n. To be defloured.
v. n. See nāthnā , v. a.
1. s. m. A nostril, a ring for the nose. نتهني چڙهانا nathne chaṛhānā, To be angry or displeased. 2. (see ناتهنا) v. n. To have the nose pierced (a bullock).

ety: nath, q.v.+nī = lī = Prk. अलिआ = S. ल+इका
def: s.f. A nose-ring (i.q. nath, q.v.); — a small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword; — a string passed through (or tied round) a bullock's nose (by which to guide him)
more: s. f. 1. A small ring worn in the nose. 2. A ring inserted in the hilt of a sword.
subs: (-kī), To be dishonoured; to be violated (a virgin).

def: s.m. (f. nātin), Daughter's son, grandson.
more: E. n. m. S. नप्त, Pālī, nattā . A daughter's son; a grandson (W. dheotā ).
s. m. Grandson, daughterʼs son. ¶ (dakh. for नप्त्री ) s. f. Granddaughter, daughterʼs daughter.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ناتی (naty /⁠nātī⁠/), from Old Hindi नांती (nāṃtī /⁠nā̃tī⁠/), from Apabhramsa णत्तिअ (ṇattia), from Prakrit 𑀡𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀅 (ṇattia), from Sanskrit नप्तृ (naptṛ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Granddaughter (i.q. nātin).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ناتی (naty /⁠nātī⁠/), from Old Hindi नांती (nāṃtī /⁠nā̃tī⁠/), from Apabhramsa णत्तिअ (ṇattia), from Prakrit 𑀡𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀅 (ṇattia), from Sanskrit नप्तृ (naptṛ) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-).

def: s.f. Bending, bowing, stooping; curvature, crookedness; — a bow; a courtesy; reverential salutation or address (i.q. namaskār); modest behaviour.
s.f. An actress, a dancer, a nāć-girl; a harlot, courtezan; — a species of fragrant plant (used in medicine); — red arsenic.

ety: for A. نتيجة natījat, v.n. fr. نتج 'to bring 'forth'
def: s.m. Fœtus; offspring; birth; — issue, fruit; consequence, result, effect, what necessarily follows, inference, conclusion (of a syllogism, &c.); moral (of a story, &c.); corollary; — sum, or substance; — retribution, reward
more: n. m. 1. End; conclusion; effect (انت, 3.) 2. ( Logic ) The inference. natījah aslī, natījah lāzmī , A natural or necessary consequence. natīje se , adj. By inference. natījah nikālnā , v. a. To infer; conclude; draw an inference. natījah k . To ensue; follow.
(from نجّ) s. m. Result, consequence, deduction, inference, conclusion, issue, what necessarily follows, retribution, reward. 2. A fœtus, offspring, birth.
subs: (-se), To draw an inference (from), to infer, to conclude
(-kā), To be the issue, or fruit, or outcome (of); to be the result or consequence (of); — to ensue (from), to follow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نتیجه (natīja), from Arabic نَتِيجَة (natīja). Compare Sindhi نتيجو (natījo).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Speaking; — rational (animal); — perspicuous (book, &c.); — imperative (order, or decree); positive, decisive, definitive, final, conclusive; — a speaker; — a rational animal.
more: n. m. نطق speaking. A rational being. nātiq , adj. Definitive; positive; decisive (قطعي). quvvat-i-nātiqah , n. f. The faculty of speech.
(part. act. of نطق) s. m. 1. Speaking, a speaker. 2. A rational being.

def: s.f. A short or light comedy (the first of the dramas of the second order); — (fem. of nāṭaka), an actress.

ety: for A. ناطقة nāt̤iqat, fem. of nāt̤iq
def: adj. Speaking, &c. (i.q. nāt̤iq, q.v.); — s.m. The faculty of speech (i.q. quwwat-ě-nāt̤iqa).
more: (part. act. fem. from نطق) adj. f. Speaking, rational. s. m. The faculty of speech.

def: s.f. = naṭnī, q.v. s.f. (colloq. or dialec.) = نتهني nathnī, q.v. s.f. A nose-ring (i.q. nath, q.v.); — a small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword; — a string passed through (or tied round) a bullock's nose (by which to guide him)
subs: (-kī), To be dishonoured; to be violated (a virgin).

ety: f. of nātī; — ˚in or ˚inī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. Daughters's daughter, granddaughter.
more: W. n. f. S. नातिन Daughter's daughter; granddaughter.
wety: From ناتی (nātī, “grandson”) + ـاِن (-in).

def: s.f. = nātin, q.v. s.f. Daughters's daughter, granddaughter.

ety: f. of nātī; — ˚in or ˚inī = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. Daughters's daughter, granddaughter.

ety: dim. of nāṭā, q.v.; — ˚iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. (rustic or dialec.) A short or diminutive man; — a bullock of an inferior stock.
n. m. 1. A bullock of an inferior stock. 2. A person of small stature.

ety: prob. S. नट+शठः, corr. to षठ
def: adj. & s.m. Naughty, wicked, mischievous; — roguish, waggish; shrewd, artful, trickish; — a naughty or mischievous child, an imp; — a rogue; a trickster, cheat.
adj. 1. Naughty; mischievous. 2. Rognish. naṭkhat , n. m. A rogue; a mischievous child.
adj. Roguish, waggish, artful, trickish, shrewd; (subst.) A cheat.

ety: the preceding + ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Naughtiness, wickedness, mischievousness, mischief; roguishness, artfulness, trickery
n. f. 1. Naughtiness. 2. Roguishness. naṭ-khaṭī k. , v. a. To be up to mischief.
s. f. Trick, roguery, artifice, artfulness.

ety: S. nata, q.v.+Prk. अलो, and अलिआ = S. ल, and ल+इका
def: adj. & s.f. (dialec.) Curved, crooked, winding, spiral, turbinate; — a snail; a cockle, &c. (i.q. nat-gullā, q.v.s.v. nata).
v.n. = naṭnā, q.v.

ety: naṭ˚ = nāṭ˚ = S. नास्ति 'it is not'+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v. int. To say 'no' (with respect to, -se), to deny, disallow, disclaim, disown; to decline, refuse.

ety: naṭ˚ = Prk. नट्ट(इ) or नट्टे(इ) = S. नर्तय(ति), fr. नर्त
def: v.n. To dance; — to strut, or mince; — to act or perform; to assume a dramatic dress or character.
Rus. v. n. To deny; disown.

v. a. To deny; disclaim; refuse. Bāṅs chaḍhat naṭnī kahe 'hot na naṭyo koë Maiṅ naṭ-ke naṭnī bhaī, naṭe so naṭnī koë .' Dohā. The acrobat the bamboo mounts and says 'let none say nay, Through 'nay' an acrobat's my fate, so all who nay will say.'

s.f. (colloq. or dialec.) = نتهني nathnī, q.v. s.f. A nose-ring (i.q. nath, q.v.); — a small ring inserted in the hilt of a sword; — a string passed through (or tied round) a bullock's nose (by which to guide him)
subs: (-kī), To be dishonoured; to be violated (a virgin).

def: s.f. The wife of a naṭ; a woman of the naṭ tribe; — a female rope-dancer, &c. (see naṭ); an actress; a nāć-girl.
more: s. f. The female of نٿ naṭ, q. v.

def: s.m. = nātā, q.v.; — 2˚ conj See s.v. nā. s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

def: part. adj. (dialec.) = nathrā, q.v.

ety: S. nata, q.v.+Prk. अलो, and अलिआ = S. ल, and ल+इका
def: adj. & s.f. (dialec.) Curved, crooked, winding, spiral, turbinate; — a snail; a cockle, &c. (i.q. nat-gullā, q.v.s.v. nata).
part. adj. (old H.) Destroyed, &c. (see nashṭ).

ety: natth or nath, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The thread or string with which a file of papers are strung together; — a record, or file (of papers) threaded together; the record or papers of a cause or suit
more: n. f. 1. The string with which papers are strung together. 2. The record or papers in a cause. natthī k. , v. a. To string together; annex; file.
subs: v.t. To string (papers) together, to file.

def: conj. (dialec.) = nā-tau, or nahīṅ-to, qq.v.
E. n. m. A dancing boy.
s.m. (rustic) A rope-dancer, &c. (i.q. naṭ, q.v.); — a dancing boy. s.m. 1˚ = nātā, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of nāth, q.v. s.m. Relative; kin; relationship; kinsmanship; alliance; affinity, consanguinity
more: s. m. Ajuggler, rope-dancer, mime, actor, &c. See نٿ naṭ.
subs: (se, or -ke sāth), To form a connection, or an alliance (with)

def: s.m. The science or art of dancing, or acting; scenic art; the union of song, pantomime, dance, and instrumental music

ety: Prk. एहाविओ, or नाविओ; S नापितः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A barber (i.q. nāʼī, q.v.). part. Having bent, or bowed, &c. (see nānā).
more: n. f. S. नौ, नौका Pālī, nāvā , Lat. navis , Gr. nau . A boat. Nāo kis ne ḍuboī, ki khuājeh Khizar ne . Prov. nāo chalānā , v. a. To steer a boat; to sail. kāgaz kī nāo , n. f. lit . a paper boat. Any frail thing.
s. f. 1. (Sans. नौः ) A boat, ship, vessel. 2. Any thing long and hollow within.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀸𑀯𑀸 (ṇāvā), from Sanskrit नौ (náu), नावा (nāvā), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *náHuṣ, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *náHuš, from Proto-Indo-European *néh₂us. Cognate with Hindi नाव (nāv), Pali nāvā, and Punjabi ਨਾਉ (nāu). Cognate through Indo-European with Ancient Greek ναῦς (naûs), Persian ناو (nâv), and Spanish nave.

def: s.f. A boat (i.q. nāʼo, the pop. form, q.v.)
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi नौ (nau), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀯 (ṇava), from Sanskrit नव (náva).

def: s.m. Species (opp. of jins, 'genus'), kind, sort; manner, mode

Relating to the new moon, of the full moon. نو خاسته nau-ḵẖāsta, or نو خيز nau-ḵẖez, Newly risen, newly sprung up, a youth. نو خيزي nau-ḵẖez-ī, s. f. or nau-duʼār (nava-dvār), adj. & s.m. Having nine doors or gates, nine-doored; — an epithet of the body (which has nine apertures or outlets, viz. the nostrils, the eyes, the ears, the mouth, the penis, and the anus); — the nine doors or outlets (of the body)
s.m. nau-rātri, s.f. or nau-rātrik, s.m. (nava-rātra, &c.), The period of nine days (from the first of the light half of the month Aśvin to the ninth) devoted to the worship of Durgā

or nau-ratan (nava-ratna), s.m. The nine precious gems (viz. a pearl, ruby, topaz, diamond, emerald, lapis lazuli, coral, sapphire, and one called Go-meda; — these nine jewels are supposed to be related to the nine planets); — a bracelet consisting of nine jewels; — the nine jewels, i.e. men of letters, at the court of Vikramāditya (viz. Dhanvantari, Kshapaṇak, Amarsingh, Śaṇku, Vetāl-bhaṭṭ, Ghaṭakarpar, Kālidās, Vararući, and Varāha-mihir); — a council of nine wise men
s.m. The aggregate of the nine kinds of pleasure (ras) recognized by the Hindu system
s.m. 'Nine and seven'; a division of the crop whereby the zamīndār takes nine parts out of sixteen, and the cultivator the remaining seven
s.m. 'Nine and seven'; the aggregate of the sixteen kinds of siṅgār (q.v.)
nau-śāyak (nava-śāyak), s.m. The designation applied to any of the nine inferior classes (viz. cow-herd, gardener, oilman, weaver, confectioner, water-carrier, potter, blacksmith, and barber)
or nau-srāddh, s.m. The first of the series of sacrifices to the manes of a deceased relative (viz. on the first, third, fifth, seventh, ninth, and eleventh days after the death)
s.m. The nine divisions or climes of the earth which constitute Jambu-dvīp, the central portion of the world, or the known world
or nau-girah, or nau-grahā (nava-grah, &c.), s.m. The nine planets (i.e. seven planets and the ascending and descending nodes)
adj. (f. -ī), Worth, or possessing, nine lacs (lākh)
s.m. The feast given in the ninth month of pregnancy
s.m. The period of nine months
or nau-nidh (nava-nidhi), s.m. The treasure of Kuver, which consists of nine fabulous gems
To be in possession of all the heart can desire
s.m. An ornament worn on the upper arm, consisting of nine precious stones set in gold or silver.

ety: for A. نوبة, v.n. of un. of ناب (for نوب
def: s.f. A period, time, turn; — access (of fever, &c.); paroxysm; fit; — occasion, opportunity; — vicissitude; — degree, pitch; weight, pass; — state (of matters or things); plight; time for recourse, or resort (to); — accident, misfortune, calamity; — relieving guard; keeping watch; — musical instruments, or drums, sounding at the gate of a great man at certain intervals; — a large kettle-drum (syn. naqqāra)
more: (from نوب Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. A period, time, turn; access (of fever, &c.) fit. 2. Relieving guard, keeping watch. 3. An accident. 4. Opportunity, occasion, vicissitude, degree, pitch, weight. 5. Instruments of musick sounding at the gate of a great man at certain intervals. نوبت جهڙنا naubat jhaṛnā, v. n. To be sounded the naubat. نوبت خانه naubat-ḵẖāna, A guard-house.
subs: or naubat jhaṛnā, The naubat to be sounded; drums, or the kettle-drum. to be beaten (at stated hours)
(-kī), Things or matters to come to such a pass or pitch; occasion for (certain things) to arrive or arise; recourse or resort was had to (e.g. laṭh-talwār-kī naubat pahuṅćī)
(kisī-kī) pahuṅćnā (yahāṅ-tak or lag), The case or state (of a person) to come (to such a pass)
s.m. A guard-house; a large room over the outer gate of a palace or a shrine where the drums or other musical instruments are sounded
s.f. The request or application for a turn (or hearing) in a court of law
naubat honā (-kī), To reach a (certain) point, to come to (such) a pass.
ex: ہماری جیت کی نوبت اب آ گئی ہے۔
hamārī jīt kī naubat ab ā gaī hai.
The conditions favourable for our victory have arrived.

def: v.t. To take into service, to employ; — v.n. To keep a servant
more: n. m. 1. A servant (چاکر). 2. A domestic; retainer; an attendant. 1. Āpkā naukar hūṅ, kuchh baiṅganoṅ kā to naukar hūṅ hī nahīṅ . Prov. I am your servant, not the brinjal's. ☞ The master was one day enjoying a mess of brinjals and extolling their excellence when the servant chimed in and said they were indeed most excellent. One day, however, the brinjals having disagreed with him he began to abuse them as a very unwholesome vegetable, and his servant then observed that they were very unwholesome truly. 'Why,' said the master, 'did you praise them before?' 'I am your servant, 'he replied,' not the brinjal's.' 2. Naukar kūkar barābar . Prov. A servant is no better than a dog. 3. Dahī kyoṅ na jamā, naukar kyoṅ na rakkhā? Riddle. Du-sukhna . Ans. Zāmin na thā . Why settled not the tyre, why engaged he not the servant? There was no rennet. (He could give no security). naukar rakhnā , v. n. T…

adj. New. نئيي سرسي naye sir se, adj. Anew, afresh. ¶ naiyā (from नौ ناو A boat) s. m. A boatman, ferryman.
subs: v.n. To cease to be new or strange; to become old or stale; — to lose the zest of novelty

v.t. To make new; to renew; renovate; restore; — to eat the first-fruits of; — to taste for the first time; — to burn (cloth, &c., — a euphemistic expression used by women instead of jalānā, or jal-jānā)
adj. Under five years old, rising five (a horse)

or naʼe-sir, or naye-sir-se, or naʼe-sire-se, adv. From the beginning; — over again; anew, afresh, de novo (syn. az sar-ě-nau); — naʼe-sire-se janam lenā, v.n. To be born again; — to obtain a new lease of life.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀯𑀺𑀅 (ṇavia), from Sanskrit नविय (náviya), नव्य (návya) (whence Bhojpuri नया (nayā)), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *náwyas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *náwyas (whence Kamkata-viri nuĩ), from Proto-Indo-European *néwyos (whence English new). Doublet of نو (no). Cognates include Punjabi نَوَاں / ਨਵਾਂ (navā̃) and Nepali नयाँ (nayā̃).

def: n. m. نوب being near. A deputy; delegate. nāyab dīvān , The deputy treasurer or accountant. nāyab munshī . An assistant writer or underclerk. nāyab munīb , A deputy and his chief. nayāb'at, nāëbī , n. f. Deputyship; agency.
more: (part. act. of نوب Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A deputy, delegate, vicegerent, attorney. نايب مناب nāʼịb manāb, A vicegerent, a locum tenens, a successor.

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), Guide, leader, conductor, chief, principal; headman, foreman, overseer (of labourers); head of a caravan; a native officer of the lowest rank (corresponding to the English 'corporal'); (in dramatic or amatory comp.) the man, husband, lover; the hero (of a drama or poem); — a person well conversant in dancing and singing; the conductor or leader of a band of musicians; — the central or chief gem (of a necklace)
more: n. m. S. नी to lead. 1. A leader; chief. 2. The head of a caravan. 3. A corporal. 4. An overseer of labourers. 5. A leader of a band of musicians.
s. m. 1. A chief, a leader, a man, a youth. 2. A native officer of the lowest rank, corresponding to corporal in our corps. 3. A person well conversant in dancing, singing, &c.
subs: adj. Under the authority of a nāyak.

def: s.f. = nāyikā, q.v. s.f. The wife of a nāyak; — a conductress, mistress; — (in erotic or dramatic poetry) a mistress; a wife; — the heroine (in a drama, &c.); — a noble lady; a damsel, lass; — the mistress of a brothel, the mother-bawd; duenna; procuress; — an inferior form or śakti of Durgā (of which there are eight, — Ugra-ćaṇḍā, Praćaṇḍā, Ćaṇḍogrā, Ćaṇḍa-nāyikā, Ati-ćaṇḍā, Ćāmuṇḍā, Ćaṇḍā, and Ćaṇḍā-vatī).
s. f. Female of ناٴي nāʼī, q. v. A barberʼs wife.

ety: Prk. एअणं, or नयणं; S. नयनं
def: s.m. The eye
subs: s.m. (lit. 'the pleasure of the sense of sight'), A kind of flower; — Indian muslin, a thick sort of jaconet muslin (plain or striped), 'Nainsook'; — an epithet of a blind person
adj. & adv. Weeping much or constantly (a woman)
vulg. nainopānt (S. nayana+up˚), s.m. The outer angle or corner of the eye, the canthus
vulg. nainotsav (S. nayana+ut˚), s.m. (f. -ā), 'Eye-festival,' any lovely or desired object; — a lamp
vulg. nainaushadh (S. nayana + osh˚), s.m. 'Eye-medicament,' green sulphate of iron (used as a collyrium).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit नयन (nayana), from the root नी (nī), from Proto-Indo-European *neyH-.

def: s.f. The pupil of the eye.
s.m. Leading, guiding, conducting, managing; governing, directing, ruling; bringing (to), drawing; — the guiding or leading organ, the eye (see next).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit नयन (nayana), from the root नी (nī), from Proto-Indo-European *neyH-.

def: s.m. pl. (Braj) Eyes.
s.m. = S نينوتسو नयनोत्सव nayanotsav, s.m. See s.v. nayan or nain.
s.m. = S نينوپانت नयनोपान्त nayanopānt, s.m. See s.v. nayan or nain.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Telingas or Gentoos (Hindūs of Southern India, com. called 'Nair') who claim the second degree of blood and antiquity of family in that nation.
s.m. = ناير nāyar, q.v. s.m. Name of a tribe of Telingas or Gentoos (Hindūs of Southern India, com. called 'Nair') who claim the second degree of blood and antiquity of family in that nation.
s.m. (old H.) A city, town (the word is still found as the termination of a few names of towns, as Bikaner, Bhaṭner, &c.).
more: n. m. S. नि below ईर flow. Water ( pānī , 1). ' Ūṅchī pāṛ samudr kī nīr jhakole le, Jal to, piyārī, khūb hai, jo koī pīvan de!' 'Āo nīche, tum pīo, tumheṅ na barje koë, Ketak kāgā pī gaë, kahā samudr thoṛo hoë?' 'The bank is high, the waves beat strong against the brink, The water, love, is nice, would any give me drink.' 'Come lower down and drink! no one will say you nay, Crows many here have drunk, exhaust it no one may.' nīr'nā , v. a. 1. To water. 2. To provide with food.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit नीर (nīra).

ety: Ap. Prk. नवएव्वउं; S. नमितव्य, rt. नम्
def: part. adj. (dialec.) Bent, bowed, &c. (syn. jhukāyā; see naunā or nawnā, and the dialec. form naynā or nainā).
s. m. A horse under five years old.
s.m. A player on the nāʼe or flute, &c.
more: s. m. A player on the (ناي nāʼe ) flute, &c.

def: s.f. The wife of a nāyak; — a conductress, mistress; — (in erotic or dramatic poetry) a mistress; a wife; — the heroine (in a drama, &c.); — a noble lady; a damsel, lass; — the mistress of a brothel, the mother-bawd; duenna; procuress; — an inferior form or śakti of Durgā (of which there are eight, — Ugra-ćaṇḍā, Praćaṇḍā, Ćaṇḍogrā, Ćaṇḍa-nāyikā, Ati-ćaṇḍā, Ćāmuṇḍā, Ćaṇḍā, and Ćaṇḍā-vatī).
more: n. f. The mistress of a brothel; the mother-bawd.
s. f. 1. Female of nāyak; a damsel, a lass. 2. The mistress of a house, particularly (now) of a brothel; a bawd.

def: s.m. Blandishment, coquetry, playfulness, amorous playfulness, feigned disdain; dalliance, toying; fondling, coaxing, soothing or endearing expression; — pride, conceit, consequential airs, whims; — softness, delicacy; elegance, gracefulness
more: n. m. 1. Blandishment; coquetry; playfulness. 2. Dalliance toying; fondling. 3. Whims; airs. nāz uṭhānā , v. n. To bear with one's whims or airs. nāz-bardār , n. m. One who bears another's whims or airs. nāz-bardārī , n. f. Bearing another's whims or airs. nāz-parvardah , n. One who has been delicately nurtured. nāz o niyāz, nāz-nakhra , n. m. Airs and graces; affectations.

s. m. 1. Blandishments, coquetry, playfulness, gracefulness, elegance, soothing, fondling, amorous playfulness, softness. 2. Pride, consequential airs. ناز اٿهاتا nāz uṭhānā, To bear with the airs or whims of another. ناز بردار nāz-bardār, One who bears the whims of another, a flatterer. ناز برداري nāz-bardār-ī, s. f. Flattery. ناز پرور nāz-parwar or ناز پرورده nāz-parwarda, Delicately brought up; a spoiled child. ناز پيشگي nāz-pesha-gī, s. f. The habit of coquetry, &c. ناز پيشه nāz-pesha, One who practises blandishments, a coquette. ناز نخرا nāz-naḵẖrā, s. m. Coquetry, dalliance. نازو نياز nāz-o-niyāz, Blandishment, toying.
subs: (-kā), To bear with the airs or whims (of another)
s.m. One who bears the airs or whims (of another); — a flatterer
s.f. Bearing the airs or whims (of); flattery
adj. & s.m. Delicately brought up; — one who has been delicately brought up; a spoilt child
s.f. The habit of coquetry, &c. (see nāz)
s.m. & f. One who practises blandishments, &c.; a coquette
or nāz-o-naḵẖra, s.m. Blandishments, coquetry; dalliance, toying; airs and graces, affectations
or nāz-o-niʻam, s.m. Greatness; ease and affluence; — the good things of the world, the blessings of life
s.m. Blandishments, &c. (i.q. nāz-naḵẖra, q.v.); — entreating soothingly, coaxing, supplication.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ناز (nāz), from Parthian nʾz (nāz, “pleasure, delight”).

ety: inf. n. of نزع 'to pull or tear out,' &c.
def: s.m. The agonies of death; the last breath; expiration.
more: n. m. The last struggle; the agonies of death ( jāṅ-kanī ).
s. m. The last breath, the agonies of death, expiration.

def: (for nāzha), s.m. contrac. of نائژه nāʼizha, q.v.

ety: for A. نظافة, inf. n. of نَظُفَ 'to be pure,' &c.
def: s.f. Cleanness, purity; neatness.

ety: for A. نزاهة, inf. n. of نَزُهَ 'to be remote (from
def: s.f. Remoteness from all evil and annoyances; — purity (of life), spotlessness.
more: (from نزه) s. f. Exemption or remoteness from any thing bad; purity.

def: s.m. pl. (of نظورة naz̤ūrat, and نظيرة naz̤īrat), Those who are looked upon or revered above all others; eminent, venerable, or respectable men; — chief men, nobles, grandees.

ety: coined from nāzuk after the model of the Ar. abst. n.
def: s.f. Softness, tenderness; — delicacy; neatness; elegance; politeness
more: n. f. 1. Softness; tenderness. 2. Delicacy; elegance. nazākat se chalnā , v. n. To tread softly or elegantly.
(from Pers. نازک nāzuk ) s. f. Delicacy, delicateness, elegance, politeness, neatness, softness.
subs: v.n. To tread softly; — to walk elegantly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَزَاکَت (nazākat).

def: adj. Proud; puffed up. nāzāṅ h. , v. n. 1. To be proud. 2. To think a great deal of oneself.

ety: imperf. part. of nāzīdan, fr. nāz
def: part. adj. Sporting, toying (as lovers); — giving oneself airs, being conceited (about), being proud; strutting, swaggering
more: (part. from نازيدن) adj. Sporting, toying (as lovers).
subs: (-par), To give ooneself airs (about), to be conceited (about), to pride oneself (on).

def: adj. Delicate; elegant; lovely; delightful; amiable, agreeable; beloved, precious; — s.f. A thin, delicate woman; a belle; a mistress, sweetheart, beloved object.
more: adj. Delicate, lovely; (subst.) A delicate woman, a mistress, a belle.

ety: inf. n. of نظر 'to look at, see, behold,' &c.
def: s.f. Sight, vision, view; look, regard, glance; observation, inspection; supervision; — favourable regard, favour, countenance; — view, opinion, estimation; — intent, design; — regard, relation, reference; — influence of an evil eye; — doubt, uncertainty, perplexity
more: n. f. See اُنکهـ 2 to 4. 1. Sight. 2. Supervision. Jo arsh se hai farsh talak ādmī meṅ hai, Dekh āṅkh khol-ke kyā kyā nahīṅ hai is meṅ! Sab kuchh isī meṅ hai, par chāhiye nazar . Nazīr. From Earth to Heaven whatever is, is found in Man, Open your eyes and see how wonderful the plan! All things in him subsist, needs only eyes to scan. 3. Countenance; favor; deference. 4. Opinion; estimation. 5. Intent; design. 6. Relation; reference. 7. The influence of an evil eye. Nazar lagnā . nazar ānā , v. n. To come in sight; to appear. Bhaṅg jab chaṛhtī hai kyā hī mazā dikhlātī hai! Makkhiyāṅ uṛtī haiṅ aur īṅṭ nazar ātī hai! How jolly you must feel when you 're with Bhaṅgaṛs. bhang well plied, When flies swarm in the air and bricks by fancy spied! nazar-andāz k. , To reject. nazar-andāzī , n. f. Valuation of land or estimate of a crop upon inspection. nazar-bāz , n. m. 1. A detective. 2. An ogler. …

s. f. 1. Sight, vision, look, regard, a glance, view, inspection, observation. 2. Doubt, perplexity. نظر آنا naz̤ar ānā, To come to sight, to appear. نظر انداز naz̤ar-andāz, adj. Rejected. نظر باز naz̤ar-bāz, 1. An ogler. 2. A thief-catcher. 3. A juggler. نظر بازي naz̤ar-bāz-ī, s. f. Ogling. نظر بچانا naz̤ar bachānā, To avoid the sight. نظربند naz̤ar-band, 1. s. m. Fascination (of conjurors). 2. adj. Strictly watched (without being imprisoned). نظربندي naz̤ar-band-ī, 1. s. f. Confinement, arrest. 2. Fascination, juggle. نظرثاني naz̤ar-i-s̤ānī, 1. A review or second examination of recruits, at which they are finally past. 2. Revisal of a writing or book. نظرسي گرا دينا naz̤ar se girā denā, To disgrace. نظر کرنا naz̤ar karnā, To look, see, behold, &c. نظرگاه naz̤ar-gāh, Theatre, amphitheatre. نظر ملانا naz̤ar milānā, To compare. نظرونسي گرنا naz̤roṅ se girnā, To be disgraced.
subs: (-ko), To come in sight or view, to appear
adj. Disregarded, unnoticed; cast off from favourable regard, rejected
v.t. To disregard, to take no notice of; to discard from favour, to reject
s.f. Valuation of land, or estimate of a crop, upon inspection
s.m. One who plays with or deceives the eyes; an ogler; a juggler; a thief-catcher; s detective

adj. Strictly watched, under surveillance; — under detention or arrest, on parole; imprisoned, confined; — s.m. A state prisoner; — causing a deception of sight; fascination of conjurors; glamour; spell, charm; — naz̤ar-band rakhnā, or karnā, v.t. To keep under surveillance; to detain (a suspected person), &c.
s.f. Surveillance; — detention; arrest; — confinement; — fascination, glamour; juggle; sleight of hand, legerdemain
(-ko), To come into view, to fall under notice; to chance to see
(ćāroṅ t̤araf), To cast glances (around), to look (all round or about)
s.m. A second inspection; revision (of a writing or book); a review or second examination of recruits (at which they are finally passed)
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To take the eye or the fancy (of)
(-par), To fix the gaze (on), cast eyes (on), to look (at), to see

(-par), To look (upon), to regard, to set eyes (upon); — to keep the eye (upon), to watch; to look (after), to attend (to); — to be intent (on); — to have in view, to contemplate, intend; — to cast a wistful eye (upon); to look sweet (upon)
(kisī-ko), To cast off; to disgrace; — to look down upon, to hold cheap
(or naz̤roṅ-se) girnā, v.n. To lose the favour or regard (of), to fall in the estimation (of, -ke), to fall into disgrace
(-par), To look (at), see, behold, view, observe, &c.
To be influenced by a malignant eye
s.f. The place where anything is kept for view or inspection; a place where any spectacle is exhibited, a theatre, ampitheatre
s.f. The influence of a malignant eye
To throw some food on the ground before eating in order to avert the malignant eye
One of the poles of the horizon
s.m. That which has been viewed with a malignant eye
(-par), To view with a malignant eye, cast an evil eye (upon), to regard with evil intent
(-ko), To fall under the gaze of a malignant eye; to be influenced by an evil eye
(-ke), To meet the gaze (of), to look (one) full in the face; — to compare
adv. In sight (of, -ke), in view; — in the view or opinion (of)
(or naz̤roṅ-meṅ) rakhnā (-ko), To keep in sight; — to keep under one's own control
(-ke, mere, &c.), To be constantly before the eyes (of); to be ever in the mind (of)
adv. With a view (to, -ke), in order (to); for; with reference (to); — according (to)
adv. With this fact in view; seeing that; whereas
adv. On a hasty or cursory view; — on the face (of); summarily
(-ko), To regard with an angry look, to frown at; — v.n. To put on an angry or a severe look
From what point of view? in what respect? why? — kaʼī naz̤ar-se, From many points of view; in many respects.
ex: آپ کی نَظَر خَراب ہوتی جا رَہی ہَیں۔
āp kī nazar xarāb hotī jā rahī ha͠i.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نظر (nazar), from Arabic نَظَر (naẓar).

def: To view.

ety: inf. n. of نظر 'to see, behold,' &c.
def: s.f. Seeing, looking (at), viewing, scanning; sight, vision.

ety: for A. نضارة, inf. n. of نضر 'to flourish,' &c.
def: s.f. Flourishing; verdancy, verdure; freshness; brightness, lustre; floridness; pleasingness; beauty.
more: (from نضر Heb. ) s. f. Freshness, verdure, beauty, pleasingness, lustre, brightness, floridness.

def: s.f. The office of a nāz̤ir, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of naz̤ar), Looks, views; inspections; — appearances.
s.m. One who looks (upon a thing or person) with a malignant eye; — one who fixes a greedy eye (upon), a greedy person.
adj. Visual; — speculative, notional, ideal, theoretical; — s.m. (in Geom.) A theorem.
s.m. The herb basil (or wild rue), Ocimum pilosum.
more: n. m. A herb; basil; wild rue; Ocymum pilosum. Palaṅg kī chūloṅ meṅ niyāzbo rakhne se khaṭmal mar jāte haiṅ .
s. m. Name of an herb, basil. (Ocimum pilosum: Roxb. ).

ety: Zend nazda, rt. nah = S. नह्; cf. नद्ध
def: adv. Near, &c. (i.q. nazdīk, q.v.). adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
more: Near, hard by, with, close to, neighbouring, contiguous, almost, about.
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.

ety: Pārsī nazdīk; Zend nazdīg, or nazdeśt; Zand nazdista, superlative of nazd; cf. S. नेदिष्ठ, superlat. of नेद
def: adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
more: adv. 1. Near ( pās ). 2. In one's view. nazdīk honā yā jānā , v. n. 1. To approach; draw near. 2. To have sexual intercourse with. Uske nazdīk hūṅ, bahut hai dūr . Wom. Jān Sāhab.
Near, hard by, with, close to, neighbouring, contiguous, almost, about.
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَزْدِیک (nazdīk).
